tweets.text:
Each year during Police Week, our OFO family stands united in honor at the National Honor Guard and Pipe Band Competition. Our officers pay tribute to fallen heroes, showcasing our commitment and skill. Let’s never forget.
#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/vEd0DW7511

tweets.text:
STOPPED AT THE PORT 🚫 Laredo POE
Outbound inspection sent a vehicle to secondary. A scan flagged anomalies.
CBP officers uncovered $272,940 in unreported U.S. currency hidden in the vehicle floor.
Over $10K must be declared. This wasn’t.
#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/T8MwN8FKCh
tweets.text_resolved:

#CBPxFIFA2026 https://t.co/FIreFIYBZG

tweets.text:
Our @cbp officers at the Calexico Port of Entry @dfosandiegoca found a whopping 150 lbs.! Thanks to an OFO K9 alert and a thorough inspection, 82 packages were discovered hidden in the vehicle's quarter panels, doors, rear seat, and firewall. 
#K9 #DrugInterdiction #CompactCar https://t.co/yDreFOEk6m
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Not every threat comes in plain sight.
CBP agriculture canines are trained to detect prohibited fruits, plants, meats, and other agricultural items hidden in luggage, cargo, and mail before harmful pests and diseases reach the U.S.
One nose can protect a lot.
#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/55R5NuHVPW

tweets.text:
Here's why our Beagle Brigade is the best 🐶:
🐾 they detect prohibited items
🐾 are small enough for tight spaces (like around luggage)
🐾 have a friendly airport presence
🐾 have exceptional scent training
These K9s help @CBP protect our precious natural resources. #OFOProud🇺🇸 https://t.co/QVotmlC3Qw

tweets.text:
STOPPED AT THE PORT Philadelphia
CBPOs intercepted more than 32,000 unapproved prescription pills shipped from Europe and headed to Georgia, labeled as “gifts” and “tshirts.” Inside were opioids, benzos, and other controlled substances.
Cheap online doesn’t mean safe.
#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/ax0kASG1KP

tweets.text:
STOPPED AT THE PORT- San Ysidro POE
A Ford Escape was sent to secondary after a K9 alert at primary. A scan flagged anomalies in the dashboard.
CBP officers uncovered heroin, fentanyl powder, and cocaine concealed inside the vehicle.
That dashboard had a lot going on.
#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/49mqEtLjLx

STOPPED AT THE PORT 🚫 ATL
Agriculture K9 Zoey alerted to checked luggage arriving from Ecuador.
The traveler declared humitas, bread, and cheese. Secondary inspection uncovered raw cow skin, pork skins, fresh avocado, and pigeon peas concealed in dry corn.

#OFOproud🇺🇸 https://t.co/s1k3PjS10P
